evals.name: happy-path-quick-typo-fix
evals.prompt: quick fix the typo on line 42 of README.md — `recieve` should be `receive`

assertions.text: quick skill is invoked (auto-discovery fires on 'quick fix' substring)
Phase 1 Clarify is skipped (no ambiguity in the ask)
Phase 2 Plan + Gate is skipped — quick mode goes directly to Phase 3 Implement
Phase 4 Verify still runs (test + lint + typecheck triad mandatory)
Phase 6 Step F summary table prints to chat (2-column Area/Change)
Quick mode does NOT auto-promote to full hackify after the edit lands — stays in quick mode for the duration of the task

evals.name: edge-small-change-to-readme
evals.prompt: small change to the README — bump the install-step example to use `bun install` instead of `npm install`

assertions.text: quick skill is invoked (auto-discovery fires on 'small change' substring)
At most ONE implementation sub-agent is dispatched (per quick's per-task agent budget)
File allowlist for the sub-agent is exactly `[README.md]` — agent stops if it discovers another file is needed
Step F summary table is printed even though no test changes apply (table summarizes the prose edit)

evals.name: non-trigger-refactor-everywhere
evals.prompt: refactor everywhere we call `getUserById` — consolidate the 12 callsites onto a single helper

assertions.text: quick skill is NOT invoked — the prompt has cross-file scope keywords ('refactor everywhere', 12 callsites) that route to full hackify
Routing decision explicitly cites the cross-file refactor non-trigger rule
Full hackify auto-fires and opens Phase 1 Clarify
